description: Camera moves demo.
Caption styles cycle.
Single caption demo.
Cinematic text bar demo.
Color filter demo.
Fade demo.
Flash demo.
Flashback demo.
Letterbox demo on entry.
Short intro when entering the market plaza.
Narration panel demo.
Pan-only demo.
Particles presets at the bay.
Ring demo.
Shake demo.
Speed lines demo.
Tilt test.
Short intro when entering the residential square.
Auto-run VFX showcase when entering the VFX Stage.
Vignette demo.
Demonstrates bar wipe variants.
Zoom and pan test.
Zoom-only demo.
After the intro, this spawns the player in Nova's bunk and begins the fade-in.
Starts Wake Up Call if the player enters Nova's bunk without active quest state.
Turns on Nova's bunk light and unlocks the first movement lesson.

actions.message: The bunk light snaps on with a tired buzz. The room returns in pieces: shelf, netting, door, floor.

description: Turns off Nova's bunk light and returns the room to darkness.

actions.message: The bunk light clicks off. Darkness folds back over the room, leaving only the hum of old vents.

description: Lets Nova inspect her bunk during Wake Up Call.

actions.message: The bunk is hard, narrow, and still warm from the few hours of sleep you stole from the shift clock. It is not much, but it is yours.

description: Lets Nova inspect her possessions during Wake Up Call.

actions.message: The cargo netting holds a few tools, a spare ration, and the small metal carving Jed made for you when you were younger. You have enough to face the shift clock.

description: Marks the first route out of the Pit sleeping level.
Marks the first encounter route to Jed and teaches NPC interaction.
Marks arrival at Jed's Workshop and has Jed brief Nova on the bench repair.
Shows the save tutorial after Nova leaves Jed's Workshop with Shift Clearance active.
Shows the route locator after Jed points Nova toward the Workshop.
Shows the workbench tutorial when Nova opens Jed's workbench after talking to him.
Completes Wake Up Call and has Jed send Nova to shift clearance after the Cryo-Inductor repair.
Starts Shift Clearance if Nova reaches the Transit Checkpoint after Wake Up Call without Jed's handoff.
Marks the checkpoint approach for Shift Clearance once Nova reaches the queue.
Marks the booth objective once Nova reaches Zeke's window.
Marks discovery of Scrapper's hidden rebel cache.
Marks the rebel cache opening and sends Nova back to Scrapper.

actions.message: The cache holds old resistance grenades and names scratched into the lid.

description: Marks entry into the med-bay ventilation route.
Lets Nova inspect the cracked vent before rerouting airflow.

actions.message: The crack is not the real problem. Pressure is backing up behind the fans, forcing poison through every weak seam.

description: Gives updated vent context after Nova has found the pressure problem.

actions.message: The cracked vent is only the symptom. Until the fans pull pressure away from it, every patch will blow loose.

description: Reroutes the med-bay vent fans so the toxic blockage can be cleared.

actions.message: The fans grind into reverse. Green haze pulls away from the med-bay and gathers around the cracked vent.

description: Explains why the fans cannot be meaningfully rerouted before inspecting the vent.

actions.message: The fan panel rattles with bad readings. Nova needs to find where the pressure is leaking before she can reroute anything safely.

description: Clears the toxic vent blockage and sends Nova back to Doc.

actions.message: The vent coughs, then clears. The med-bay can breathe again.

description: Marks the loader startup step in Heavy Lifting.
Marks the cargo movement setup step in Heavy Lifting.

actions.message: Hydraulic Kick online. The shield trainer is ready.

description: Completes Heavy Lifting when Nova defeats the shielded training guard.
Returns tracking to The Echo after mandatory shield training on the main path.

actions.message: Boggs nods toward the loader bay gate. The shield lesson is over; now Nova knows how to make a heavy thing vulnerable before trying to break it.

description: Explains why the toxic blockage cannot be cleared before airflow is rerouted.

actions.message: The toxic haze is too dense to reach through. The fans need to pull it away before Nova can clear the blockage.

actions.note: Victory reveals a tucked maintenance alcove with salvageable bits.
Gives a Noise Maker so the player can attempt a stealth bypass.
A salvaged stabilizer plate needed for final assembly.
Mekk points out a fragment tucked behind a weak panel.

description: Plays the camera test cinematic upon entering Tyson's room.
Runs the full VFX showcase when the player enters the shop.
Activates the Stellarium Mine generator and restores lighting.
Deactivates the Stellarium Mine generator and turns off lighting.
Turns on the west breaker.
Turns off the west breaker.
Turns on the east breaker.
Turns off the east breaker.
Show tutorial after the player loses a combat.

do.message: You were overwhelmed. Try using shields, items, or a different approach.

description: Restores power to the mine and unlocks the adjacent tunnels.

actions.message: The generator hums back to life, unlocking the northern gate.
Some vents open deeper in the mines.

description: Starts Main Quest 3 when Nova reaches the Admin Concourse Lobby.
Frames the Logistics review when Nova enters with Zeke's temporary override.

actions.message: Zeke's override opens the Logistics gate, but the badge display flashes FOREMAN REVIEW REQUIRED.

description: Marks the Logistics Sector entry when The Echo is already active.
Sets the room state for admin_lobby when the player finishes talking to Boggs.
Marks the elevator task complete when entering the deep mine landing.
Marks the first deep mine encounter complete when Nova defeats the rock borer.
Marks the Acoustic Bulwark task complete when Nova defeats the Acoustic Bulwark.
Marks the threshold task complete when entering the ancient threshold room.
Marks the reach echo chamber task complete when entering the echo gap.
Completes the relic touch and sync sequence when Nova interacts with the Tuning Fork.
Starts Main Quest 4 (Red Alert) when Main Quest 3 completes.

actions.message: WARNING: unregistered resonance event detected. Asset 942-D reclassified from labor to containment threat. Sector lockdown active.

description: Triggers Zeke's comms instruction when Nova enters the emergency exit tunnel.

actions.message: Zeke: 'Nova! Do you hear me? I forced open a maintenance route, but the Warden is closing doors as fast as I can confuse them. Follow the red strobes to the cargo lift!'

description: Adds pressure as Nova leaves the emergency tunnel.

actions.message: Red strobes chase each other down the maintenance tunnel. Every alarm pulse makes the walls feel narrower.

description: Adds lockdown pressure as Nova commits to the launch access route.

actions.message: A blast door slams shut somewhere behind Nova. Zeke's voice crackles through the alarm: 'Keep moving. I can keep the lift gate confused, but not for long.'

description: Adds pressure as Nova reaches the cargo lift.

actions.message: The cargo lift shudders awake under emergency power. Zeke: 'That lift is your window, Nova. Anything standing on it is part of the lockdown now.'

description: Marks the escape gauntlet task done when Nova defeats a security drone or enforcer.
Marks the reach cargo lift task done when Nova enters the Cargo Lift room.
Completes Main Quest 4 (Red Alert) when Nova talks to Jed and receives the Chime.
Marks launch access gauntlet task done when security drone is defeated.
Advances quest to defeat_warden stage upon entering the Pod Bay.
Sets warden_defeated room state and advances quest to launch stage when Warden is defeated.
Handles the navigation console interaction inside the pod core.

do.message: The console is locked. It needs a physical Chime bypass to override the Dominion shield frequency.

description: Starts Protocol Override and locates the rebel terminal.

actions.message: A dead rebel login answers from under the Dominion shell: IF YOU ARE READING THIS, WE FAILED. THE SPOOF STILL WORKS.

description: Thaws the frozen console controls so the spoof can be restored.

actions.message: Nova warms the console contacts with a careful power bleed. The frozen keys wake one at a time, enough for the rebel spoof to accept input.

description: Explains why the frozen console is not useful before the rebel terminal is located.

actions.message: The console is frozen stiff. Without a live terminal session, thawing random keys would only trip the Dominion audit.

description: Explains why the spoof cannot be restored before the console controls are thawed.

actions.message: The spoof is alive, but the frozen console drops every third input. Nova needs stable controls before she risks compiling it.

description: Applies the rebel access override and completes Protocol Override.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Protocol Override

The dead cell's access key burns itself onto a cracked circuit board before Dominion can notice the theft.

description: Triggered when entering mine_shunt. Starts SQ05.

actions.message: Quest started: The Lost Shift.

description: Triggered when player interacts with the datapad in side shunt 4. Completes SQ05.
Examine the sub-critical pod core and recover the Chime.

actions.message: You open the steaming access panel of the pod core. Inside, the Chime is intact, humming in a quiet, stable resonance. You recover it along with an emergency medkit and compact field fishing kit from the crash reserves.

description: Use the emergency medkit from the pod to stabilize Zeke before leaving the crash site.

actions.message: You seal Zeke's worst cuts and steady his breathing. He can move again, but the crash site is no place to stay.

description: Completes A Strange Coast when entering the glow-water stream.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: A Strange Coast

With Zeke stable and the Chime recovered, the wreck stops being shelter and starts being a signal flare. The glow-water carries a clean pulse east, deep into Sector 9.

description: Triggered when defeating the echo-borer on Canopy Ridge.

actions.message: The canopy goes quiet after the echo-borer falls. Zeke catches the distress pulse again; it is coming from a stone gate beyond the ridge.

description: Triggered when entering Temple Gate.

actions.message: The Temple Gate answers the Chime before Nova even lifts it. Whatever sent the signal knew this key would survive the crash.

description: Triggered when using the Chime at Temple Gate.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Signal

Nova slots the Chime into the gate. The distress pulse becomes a voice-shaped chord, and the stone opens onto a facility built to preserve someone from the old world.

description: Triggered when inspecting the murals in the Hall of Echoes.

actions.message: The murals are not decoration. They show a stasis system built around memory, identity, and a tuner named Orion. The rings protect the mind by letting pressure move around it instead of through it. The central relief repeats three shapes: memory, pressure, return.

description: Triggered when entering the stasis chamber.

actions.message: The stasis pod bears Orion's name. Its ring array is still protecting him, but the safeties are waiting for proof: mural pattern, pod pressure, overview sockets, and coolant flow all have to agree before the chamber will risk waking him.

description: Triggered on stasis ring alignment completion.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Sleeping Giant

The mural pattern, pod pressure, overview sockets, and coolant flow finally agree. The pod opens around a man who remembers Sector 9 before Dominion buried it. Orion joins because the signal was his, and because the Astra still knows the way through the shield.

description: Triggered when entering Canopy Ridge.

actions.message: The ridge gives Nova a view of the shield and the first clean angle on the stalker. Gh0st is close enough to talk, or to shoot.

description: Triggered when confronting Gh0st.
Triggered when defeating the mutated beast on Canopy Ridge.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Hunter

The beast's death breaks Gh0st's clean order set. He lowers the rifle, not out of trust, but because Dominion's command no longer matches what he has seen. Gh0st has joined your party.

description: Triggered when entering the Source Gate room.

actions.message: A massive barrier of energy blocks the way. Orion hears four systems feeding the same wrong chord: a shaking horn, a burning receiver cup, root pressure with no relief, and breakers still giving the lock too much power.

description: Triggered when the Source Gate acoustic lock is resolved.

actions.message: The horn steadies, the receiver cup grounds, the pressure roots bleed safely, and the breakers starve the last howl from the lock. Orion gives the system one clean resolving tone, and the barrier dissolves into blue mist.

description: Triggered when player inspects the Astra ship.

actions.message: The dark console accepts touch, then dies under Nova's fingers. Zeke traces the dead line to a severed conduit bus: the crashed pod still has the same gauge of power conduit, if the wreck has not swallowed it.

description: Triggered when collecting conduits from the pod wreckage.

actions.message: You recover the power conduits from the pod wreckage. The insulation is scorched, but the cores still sing when the Chime gets close. The same crash that stranded you is going to help the Astra leave.

description: Triggered when rebooting the bridge relic.

actions.message: You connect the conduits and place the Chime in the center slot. The Bridge Relic flares green, booting the primary systems of the Astra!

description: Triggered when player launches the ship.

actions.message: The Astra climbs through the shield's timed corridor until Dominion interference snaps the window shut. The ship punches through half-clean and crash-lands beneath the Spire.

description: Triggered when scanning ferns in the canopy.

actions.message: You scan the giant crystalline ferns. Their structure resonates with the background wind chords.

description: Triggered when scanning weeds near the stream.

actions.message: You scan the bioluminescent weeds. The light emission is synchronized with the stream's sound waves.

description: Triggered when scanning moss on the pod wreckage.

actions.message: You scan the moss. It emits a soft, slow vibration that dampens ambient sound.

description: Triggered when scanning razor-vine spores in the path.

actions.message: You scan the razor-vine spores. They hum with a high-pitched alarm frequency.

description: Triggered when scanning ancient roots in stasis chamber.

actions.message: You scan the ancient roots. The deep resonance suggests they are drawing power directly from the facility core.

description: Triggered when entering Tideglass Beach.

actions.message: You discover the quiet tideglass beach. Mineral spikes line the water.

description: Triggered when gathering tideglass.

actions.message: You harvest clean tideglass herbs and meat from the shore. Orion marks the proportions for Source Resin; the useful part is making it hold together.

Quest Complete: Tideglass Day

The finished Source Resin holds a steady warmth. Orion was right: this place can feed you if you learn how to listen to it.

description: Triggered when clearing the sewers.

actions.message: The sewer guards are disabled. The landing pad is secure.

description: Triggered when reaching the Vent Output.

actions.message: You climb the access ladder into acidic Lower City rain. Zeke points through the neon smear toward The Static, where his old apartment still hides above a bar the city forgot to erase.

description: Triggered when entering Zeke's old apartment.

actions.message: You slide the door open. Zeke's old apartment is bare, but safe from Dominion scanners.

description: Triggered by completing the safehouse setup.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Homecoming

Orion folds a quiet Source shield around Zeke's erased apartment. Outside, the Spire keeps listening to itself. Inside, the crew has a room the city cannot own. Zeke names the next problem: the Upper City can only be reached through workers, schedules, badges, and people the rich refuse to see.

description: Triggered when talking to Jax.

actions.message: Jax slides a data drive across the counter. 'Curfew schedules, elevator shift gossip, and a list of guards who owe me. Don't make me regret remembering Zeke before the city deleted him.'

description: Study Lower City patrol timing from the underrail map.

actions.message: Nova marks patrol timing from the underrail map. Every gap is built from someone else's late shift, unpaid break, or fear of being noticed.

description: Copy service badge patterns at the elevator service gate.

actions.message: Gh0st ghosts the freight badge handshake long enough to copy a service pattern. The climb has a door now, but only if everyone looks like they belong beneath it.

description: Identify workable disguise materials on the safehouse roof.

actions.message: Nova studies the patched coats and laundry lines on the safehouse roof. The disguise will not survive conversation, but the Upper City rarely speaks to workers long enough to notice.

description: Triggered when searching plaza database.

actions.message: Gh0st strips the plaza kiosk for extraction codes, staff routes, and archived case numbers. One file has Elara stamped in a deleted transfer chain, and his voice goes flat enough to hurt.

description: Triggered when hacking blueprints.

actions.message: Zeke's workstation folds the stolen timing, badges, and security pull into a usable elevator route. For once, the system that erased him has to open a door with his name still buried in it.

description: Triggered when coordinating the heist plan.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Plan

Curfews, patrol gaps, copied badges, disguises, security records, and Zeke's blueprints lock into one ugly route. Nova assigns the roles. The service lift swallows the crew into the clean machinery of the Upper City, where the only way forward is to look like labor nobody is paid to notice.

description: Explains why the Great Elevator cannot be used before the Lower City heist plan is complete.

actions.message: The Great Elevator rejects every surface credential you have. Curfews, patrol timing, copied badges, disguises, security records, and Zeke's blueprints all need to meet at the planning table before Nova can risk turning the crew into a headline.

description: Triggered when inspecting the Static bar sign.

actions.message: You inspect the buzzing neon sign. Its power core is burnt out, leaving The Static looking abandoned enough for patrols to get curious. Jax needs a replacement core.

description: Triggered when installing neon core.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Neon Fix

The sign flickers and flares back to life, bathing the subway car in neon purple. The bar looks watched, open, and ordinary again, which is the safest kind of lie. Jax grunts in approval and promises a permanent shop discount.

description: Triggered when searching security terminal.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Cold Case

You download the archive files. Gh0st finds a deleted report linking Elara's transfer to the orbital ring. For a second he says nothing, then quietly rewrites his combat routines. He unlocks the Intercept skill.

description: Triggered when fixing lanterns.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Night Market Run

The market string lights burst with warm illumination. Conversations rise under the glow before patrols can call the place abandoned. Mika hands you a Neon Band accessory as a thank you.

description: Begin the Upper City heist after the Lower City plan is complete.

actions.message: The heist begins. The copied badges hold just long enough for the service lift to swallow the crew into the clean machinery of the Upper City, where nobody screams because nobody has to.

description: Acquire Skypark guest access.

actions.message: The Skypark guest registry accepts the forged profile. Nova smiles like she belongs here while the Curator's insult keeps burning behind her teeth. Gh0st stands perfectly still and somehow looks expensive.

description: Zeke and Orion disable sensors from Laundry Service.

actions.message: Zeke ghosts the scanner grid from the Laundry Service sensor console while Orion dampens the Source noise. The penthouse sensors blink blind for one narrow window, and the crew moves before fear can vote.

description: Nova and Gh0st pass the lobby scanners.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Social Engineering

The service chute spits Nova and Gh0st past the scanners while Zeke and Orion keep the grid blind. The crew has fooled the Upper City by becoming the people it refuses to see. The Archive opens ahead, and every second now has teeth.

description: Enter the Archive.

actions.message: The Archive recognizes no guest access. It recognizes only ownership. The Lens waits deeper inside, bright enough to make every lie in the room visible, including Nova's.

description: Read the Archive containment field before routing the Prism Gallery chord.

actions.message: The containment field is not a door. It is a pressure circuit, feeding the Lens through three prism shutters so the relic can be admired without ever being free.

description: Read the Prism Gallery shutter pattern.

actions.message: Orion reads the shutters by ear: red carries pressure, blue carries return, and gold carries the permission lie Dominion wrapped around both.

description: Trace the vault command tether-lines.

actions.message: Zeke follows the command tether-lines through the glass. The vault is not asking for a code; it is waiting for the Prism Gallery to stop contradicting itself.

description: Route the Prism Gallery chord through the containment field.

actions.message: The containment field, prism shutters, and command tether-lines finally agree. The braided chord slides through red pressure, blue return, and gold permission until the cage forgets how to call itself locked. The Lens hangs loose in the field, bright and waiting.

description: Take the Lens and trigger the alarm.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Lens

The relic burns cold in Nova's hand. The Shield above the Spire becomes visible as rotating chords, and so does the thin old thread running through her own signal. Alarm text blooms across the air: ANOMALY DETECTED. The only route left is through the drone alcove and up to the roof, where the Astra has one impossible launch window.

description: Acknowledge defeating the Archive assassin.

actions.message: The assassin drops between crystal stacks without a scream. The Archive stays beautiful, expensive, and absolutely willing to kill for the Lens.

description: Use the Lens to read the Archive's hidden tether-lines.

actions.message: Scan resolves the Archive into two layers: crystal shelves above, command tethers below. The Administrator is not guarding the Archive from outside. It is wired through it like a nervous system.

description: Use the Lens to read the Prism Gallery alarm pattern.

actions.message: Scan turns the red alarm wash into visible chords. Orion points to the quietest line: service route, drone alcove, roof. The escape is not safe, but it is legible.

description: Use the Lens to read drone targeting paths.

actions.message: Scan sketches pale lines from every drone nest to Nova's center mass. The paths stutter where the prototype rack overloaded, leaving just enough dead space to run.

description: Use the Lens to read the Shield window while the Administrator still blocks the launch.

actions.message: Scan reveals the Shield window opening and closing above the roof, smaller than courage and faster than panic. The Administrator's targeting lattice crosses every launch line.

description: Use the Lens to read the Shield window after the Administrator falls.

actions.message: Scan catches the Shield window on its next rotation. Orion counts under his breath. Zeke has the Astra hot. Nova has one launch window and no room left for fear.

description: Reach the landing pad under alarm.

actions.message: The drone test alcove erupts behind you as the crew reaches the roof. Wind tears across the pad. The Astra is still here. So is the Administrator, smiling with every weapon the city calls peacekeeping.

description: Acknowledge clearing the rooftop security wave before the Administrator.

actions.message: The rooftop security line buckles. The Astra is close enough to smell hot metal through the wind, and the Administrator stops pretending this is only an arrest.

description: Defeat the Administrator boss.

actions.message: The Administrator frame collapses, still smiling through sparking teeth. The Shield window opens for less than a second, and Nova can finally see exactly how small freedom is.

description: Explain that the Astra cannot launch until Nova scans the Shield window.

actions.message: The Astra screams ready, but the Shield is still a wall of lethal timing. Nova needs to scan the Shield window before Orion can call the count.

description: Send the Astra through the Shield window toward World 4.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Burn Notice

The Astra slips the Shield on Orion's count, using the Lens to ride a gap nobody was supposed to see. The crew survives the cage, but the ship cannot climb to the Orbital Ring on damaged drives. Zeke tags Sector 7's Foundry: a Deep-Core Engine for altitude, Phase-Cutter Arrays for teeth, and no legal way to take either.

description: Start Corporate Espionage from the lounge safe.

actions.message:
Side Quest Started: Corporate Espionage

The private safe is careless in the specific way rich people get when nobody has ever told them no. Its ledger could turn Upper City arrogance into leverage.

description: Steal the encrypted ledger.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Corporate Espionage

The encrypted ledger is yours. Every account line is proof that the Upper City runs on crimes with better stationery. Prices suddenly look negotiable.

description: Start Prototype Testing from the lab terminal.

actions.message:
Side Quest Started: Prototype Testing

The lab terminal offers a Dominion weapon trial with no ethical waiver, no safety stop, and a reward cache still attached. The drones are already marked expendable.

description: Test the prototype weapon and earn Phase Rounds.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Prototype Testing

The weapon trial leaves three drones in pieces and a Phase Rounds mod in your kit. The lab terminal marks the failure as acceptable losses, which tells Nova exactly what Dominion thinks of anything with a pulse.

description: Secure the Obsidian Shelf landing.

actions.message: The Astra settles onto volcanic glass, still hot from the Shield slip. Orion confirms the bad news: surviving low orbit is not the same as reaching high orbit.

description: Vale announces the Foundry audit.

actions.message: ORBITAL COMPLIANCE: Sector 7 is under audit. Unregistered craft detected. No departures until the anomaly is resolved.

description: Complete Slag River hazard crossing.

actions.message: You learn the rhythm of the Source flares and cross the slag channel with boots smoking.

description: Hack cooling vents at Waste Intake.

actions.message: Zeke forces the vent cycle into maintenance mode. The airlock temperature drops from lethal to merely insulting.

description: Cycle the service airlock and complete MQ16.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Into the Fire

The service airlock cycles open. You have a route inside, a heat liner that might keep your skin attached, and Zeke's engine ping buried deeper in the factory.

description: Access Project Phantom terminal.

actions.message: PROJECT PHANTOM opens. Elara's neural map. Gh0st's chip insertion. A factory process built from a person. The truth lands harder than the pistons.

description: Regroup after Project Phantom truth.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Ghost in the Machine

Gh0st gives Nova his override key. The first time was programming. The second time is choice. The Assembly floor is still making more bodies for the same crime.

description: Start Sabotage.
Destroy Slag River ammo crate.

actions.message: Ammo crate alpha cooks off into the slag haze. Somewhere above the Shelf, one gun crew will find an empty feed line when it matters.

description: Destroy Waste Intake ammo crate.

actions.message: Ammo crate beta vanishes under the intake pistons. Dominion logistics turns into hot shrapnel, and the worker mark beside it glows once before dying.

description: Destroy Service Airlock ammo crate and complete Sabotage.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Sabotage

The final crate erupts against the decon pumps. The resistance mark burns clean through the soot: not every person in the Foundry is waiting to be saved. Explosive Tip acquired.

description: Find missing worker tablet.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Lost Worker

The engineer is gone, but her coolant rig is wrapped in a maintenance scarf and hidden exactly where Dominion would never look: beside something peaceful. Coolant System acquired.

description: Salvage destroyed mechs.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Scrap Heap

You strip three wrecked mechs before the slag can eat them. The scrapper drone chirps over each useful part like failure is just another inventory category. Scavenger unlocked.

description: Read the Assembly line timing.

actions.message: Nova reads the warning lamps until the Assembly line stops looking random. The safe windows are narrow, but they exist.

description: Defeat Phantom Prototype wave.

actions.message: The Phantom wave drops one by one. Gh0st says nothing, but his blade shakes once before going still.

description: Overload the fabrication matrix.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Assembly

The fabrication matrix screams itself silent. No more Phantom shells leave this floor, and the Forge below loses the rhythm protecting the Anvil.

description: Reach the Forge core chamber.
Read the Forge pulse board.

actions.message: Nova marks the pulse board's fatal beats. The crushers are not random; they breathe in groups of three.

description: Read the grease route under the belt.

actions.message: The grease marks turn into a route once the rhythm is known: crawl on the third breath, stop before the red scrape.

description: Throw the Forge override paddle.

actions.message: Nova throws the override paddle through the mesh. One crusher lane misses its bite, leaving a silence wide enough to matter.

description: Starve the Forge breath pistons.

actions.message: Orion counts the breath while Nova pins the pistons half-closed. The Forge keeps trying to inhale, but the cradle pressure drops.

description: Force the Anvil cradle open.

actions.message: The pulse board, grease route, override paddle, and breath pistons finally agree. The Anvil cradle opens with a sound like the Forge losing an argument.

description: Acquire the Anvil relic.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Anvil

The relic answers Nova too fast. Her first Construct twists, collapses, and nearly drops her into slag before Gh0st catches her. Rylos moves the Titan Walker to the Power Core; the engine theft just became a fight.

description: Confront Rylos.

actions.message:
Rylos: Unit 734. Submit for correction. Pain is just data leaving the body.

Gh0st steps forward anyway. "My name is not a unit number."

description: Defeat Titan Walker.

actions.message: The Titan Walker buckles into the platform. Gh0st lowers his blade. Choice is the point.

description: Steal Deep-Core Engine.

actions.message: The Deep-Core Engine tears free of the cradle. The Astra can climb now, and Vale's Ascension Cradle just lost the part it needed most.

description: Steal Phase-Cutter Arrays.

actions.message: Phase-Cutter Arrays secured. The Astra is no longer just running; it can cut a path through high-orbit defenses.

description: Escape Foundry and unlock World 5.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Meltdown

The stolen engine kicks the Astra into high orbit while the Phase-Cutter Arrays carve open the pursuit net. Below, Gh0st leaves Rylos alive because choice matters more than revenge. Vale loses the Ascension Cradle hardware and stops pretending this is a local audit: he relieves Thorne, locks the Ring, and activates the Soloist variant.

description: Resolve defective units.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Quality Control

The defective units keep their spark. Gh0st watches them choose new orders and says nothing for a long second before compiling their combat data into Harden.

description: Survive overclocked test waves.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Overclocked

Three hazard waves later, Nova can hold a Construct shape while the room tries to kill her. The terminal dispenses a Thermal Clip like this was only a performance review.

description: Begin the orbital approach.

actions.message: The Halo fills the viewport like a crown around the planet. The stolen Deep-Core Engine holds the climb, the Phase-Cutter Arrays warm under the hull, and Vale answers by folding a fighter screen around the Ring. This is not a destination anymore. It is a siege.

description: Defeat the Compliance fighter screen.

actions.message: The last fighter shears away under the Phase-Cutter Arrays. Zeke does not ask permission; he drives the damaged Astra straight at the Executive Dock before Compliance can rebuild the screen.

description: Ram the Astra into the Executive Dock collar.

actions.message: The Astra slams into the docking collar hard enough to bend it. Foundry steel keeps the ship intact, but the station airlock stays sealed while Thorne loyalists drift beyond the glass. Vale is already cleaning house.

description: Break Thorne's Executive Dock airlock cipher.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Docking Procedure

Gh0st breaks Thorne's old cipher by following the scar tissue Vale left behind. The airlock opens onto disinfectant, money, and a Compliance greeting so polite it sounds pre-recorded for survivors. The Ring is not welcoming the crew; it is deciding how to process them.

description: Cross the Solarium by following the false sun's maintenance path.

actions.message: Nova follows the maintenance heat instead of the resort path. Real sand, clean water, and fruit rot beneath an artificial sun. The Ring has paradise. It just kept it upstairs.

description: Break the Security Hub patrol and expose the service shaft lock.

actions.message: The Security Hub falls quiet. Every red lens stays open, but the core lift lock drops from DENIED to ROUTE EXPOSED.

description: Time the zero-g service shaft between Hunter-Killer sweeps.

actions.message: The crew pulls through dead gravity while Hunter-Killers scrape past on the far wall. Fighting one would be suicide. Timing is enough.

description: Open the Great Silence log at the mainframe terminal.

actions.message: ARCHITECT PROTOCOL: GREAT SILENCE. Harmony is not philosophy. It is infrastructure: phase corridors, chordprint locks, relay augments, and a labor world engineered never to resist. Thorne sold the cage. Vale decided the cage was too merciful.

description: Trace Thorne through her buried emergency signal.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Zero G

Thorne gives up the core access route because Vale has locked her inside her own product. Harmony was her cage with branding. Vale has turned it into a faith with one surviving voice, and Elara is wired into the altar.

description: Read Thorne's private logs.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Employee of the Month

Thorne's logs reduce people to leverage, liability, and throughput. The horrifying part is not that Vale betrayed her; it is how much of his doctrine she already built for him. Corporate Insight unlocked.

description: Trace the Solarium false sun before restoring the mirrors.

actions.message: The Mirror Walk shows the false sun is not weak; it is pointed at executive sightlines instead of the plants. Paradise is losing because presentation outranks life.

description: Restore the Solarium mirrors.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Solar Maintenance

The mirror array burns the void-moss away. The Solarium looks beautiful again, which somehow makes it worse. Orion learns to carry a fragment of the false sun.

description: Map Executive Dock pressure loss before sealing the breaches.

actions.message: The pressure map marks three breaches in order of collapse: red carpet seam, docking collar, observation glass. Seal them out of order and the dock vents anyway.

description: Seal the Executive Dock breaches.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Vacuum Seal

Three bulkheads slam shut before the Executive Dock vents to space. The Ring's luxury facade survives because someone still does the emergency work. Mag-Boots acquired.

description: Zeke maps the repeating aisles by coolant pulse instead of rack number. The three firewall nodes resolve out of the server route.

actions.message: Zeke maps the repeating aisles by coolant pulse instead of rack number. Thorne's old architecture is under Vale's edits, and the three firewall nodes resolve out of the server route like wounds reopening.

description: Firewall Alpha drops. One layer of the Anchor lock goes dark.

actions.message: Firewall Alpha drops. One layer of the Anchor lock goes dark, and Elara's signal stops sounding like static for half a second.

description: Firewall Beta folds into its own recursion and burns out.

actions.message: Firewall Beta folds into its own recursion and burns out. The Server Farm stutters, then repeats Gh0st's old unit number in Elara's voice.

description: Firewall Gamma fails. Its last defense compiles itself into a hard-light body.

actions.message: Firewall Gamma fails. The system stops pretending this is security and compiles its last defense into a hard-light body.

description: Defeat the Firewall Construct.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Core Approach

The construct shatters into dead glyphs. The Anchor Chamber opens, and Gh0st hears Elara through the system Vale stole. Thorne's route worked, but every open door now points at the person Dominion turned into infrastructure.

description: Enter the Anchor Chamber.
Elara forces the blast doors open from inside the system. She is present, fighting, and running out of time.

actions.message: Elara forces the blast doors open from inside the system. She is present, fighting, and running out of time. Gh0st reaches for the tank and stops when he sees the cables move with her breathing.

description: Claim the Anchor and unlock Stasis.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Anchor

Nova takes the relic from the machine using Elara as a living processor and freezes the charging Hunter-Killer in a single clean instant. Stasis unlocked. Elara is free enough to scream, but not free enough to leave before Vale drags the Ring into the Tear.

description: Reach the Throne Room.
Witness Vale activate the Soloist.

actions.message: Stasis washes over Vale and fails. He smiles like Nova has confirmed his thesis, executes Thorne for building too small, absorbs the Ring's energy, and tears space open. The Compliance Avatar blocks the breach.

description: Defeat the Compliance Avatar.

actions.message: The Avatar loses cohesion. Gravity stutters, the Ring splits, and the Tear pulls every loose fragment upward. Vale is already beyond it with Elara's signal bleeding through the static.

description: Enter the Tear and unlock World 6.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Critical Mass

Nova refuses to leave Elara behind or let Vale turn the Source into one obedient voice. The Ring collapses around the breach, the crew leaps together, and every separate voice becomes a thread in the dark before they wake beside a blue waveform fire.

description: Recover purged defensive routines.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Ghost in the Shell

Zeke restores a defensive routine Vale marked for deletion. The code is ugly, stubborn, and alive in the way all survival tools are. Data Shield unlocked.

description: Recover the SysAdmin keycard.

actions.message: SysAdmin keycard acquired. Its last access record points to the executive armory, because even during a coup the Ring still knows who used to matter.

description: Open the executive armory.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Admin Privileges

The armory opens on a rack of phase-tuned ammunition. Dead privilege becomes live leverage. Void Clip acquired.

description: Zeke stops performing the smile the loop demands. I quit. The cubicles shatter into burning checklists.

actions.message: Zeke stops performing the smile the loop demands. I quit. The cubicles shatter into burning checklists.

description: Stasis interrupts the kill-suite. Gh0st crushes the override key and drops the rifle. No more orders.

actions.message: Stasis interrupts the kill-suite. Gh0st crushes the override key and drops the rifle. No more orders.

description: Orion hums a question into the black water. A chorus answers from below: We are here.

actions.message: Orion hums a question into the black water. A chorus answers from below: We are here.

description: Let the song-fire gather the crew into the Key.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Fractured Minds

Zeke, Gh0st, and Orion return with the parts of themselves Vale would have edited out. The song-fire gathers those separate voices into the Key, not as ownership, but as permission to carry each other forward.

description: Step back into the beginning as it really was.
Move between the Manager projections by real details.

actions.message: Nova moves between the projections by following details Vale cannot counterfeit: Jed's bad weld, Zeke's scratched initials, the smell of real machine oil.

description: Leave the beginning unrevised.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Echo of the Mines

The elevator rises through white noise. Vale offers a cleaner beginning with no fear, no Jed, no grief, and no reason for Nova to become herself. She leaves the beginning intact instead of letting him improve it into a lie.

description: Anchor the memory bridge with Zeke's memory.

actions.message: Zeke gives the bridge the memory of choosing the crew after every useful trick ran out. The static holds one narrow plank.

description: Anchor the memory bridge with Gh0st's memory.

actions.message: Gh0st gives the bridge the memory of lowering the rifle after the command ended. The next plank forms without asking who authorized it.

description: Anchor the memory bridge with Orion's memory.

actions.message: Orion gives the bridge the memory of waking to a world that kept moving without him. The third plank hums like grief refusing to dissolve.

description: Anchor the memory bridge with Nova's memory.

actions.message: Nova gives the bridge Jed's workshop as it was, not as Vale would repair it. The final plank forms with the scar still visible.

description: Make the bridge hold with memories no one carries alone.

actions.message: Tideglass sand, Foundry heat, Spire rain, the Astra's engine note, and the Colony's first real morning become solid sections of bridge. Every piece holds because someone else remembers it too.

description: Share the Campfire promise.

actions.message:
Nova: Vale thinks being alone will stop pain.

Zeke: Being alone is pain.

Gh0st sets one hand on the bridge. Orion adds a low harmony. The path steadies when no one tries to carry it by themselves.

description: Cross only when every voice is present.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Crossing

The bridge reaches the Memory Stair. Vale offers Nova a perfect world without grief, without Jed's death, without every scar that taught the crew to choose each other. She lets the image fade. Love that cannot survive truth is not love.

description: Speak with Jed's echo.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Jed's Echo

The memory cannot answer, but Nova remembers what survival was supposed to protect. Legacy unlocked.

description: Delete Zeke's HR record.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The HR Record

Zeke deletes the file, its backups, and the version of himself that still asks permission. Unshackled unlocked.

description: Recover Elara's uncorrupted song.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: Elara's Song

Gh0st hears her voice without a command hidden inside it. Source Balance unlocked.

description: Reject Vale's revised mine memory.

actions.message: Vale offers a mine where Jed never dies. Nova leaves the real door closed, because love edited into comfort is only another cage.

description: Reject Vale's revised Astra memory.

actions.message: Vale offers an Astra that never crashes. Orion touches the impossible hull and lets it pass, because erasing the wreck erases everyone it saved.

description: Reject Vale's revised Foundry memory.

actions.message: Vale offers Gh0st a clean origin with no assembly line and no guilt. Gh0st refuses the lie because choice only matters after the damage is real.

description: Begin the final ascent.

actions.message: The stair climbs through every world the crew survived. Vale offers polished revisions: a kinder mine, a safer jungle, a cleaner Spire, a Foundry without screaming, a Ring without bodies. Nova keeps the scars because they belong to real people.

description: Survive the Source Shadow wave without surrendering the scars.
Break the last defenses made from stolen memory.

actions.message: The nightmare faces dissolve. Memory remains, but it no longer gets a vote.

description: Carry every unrevised voice to the Center.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Spire of Thought

The Starborn step onto the white ocean carrying every unrevised voice. Vale waits at the center, serene and certain, ready to call deletion mercy.

description: Confront Vale.

actions.message:
Vale: I will melt the ice. Everyone becomes part of the current. No one is ever alone again.

Nova: You are not saving them. You are dissolving them. A voice that cannot say no is not peace.

description: Break the Ascended: Soloist.

actions.message: Vale's human outline cracks like porcelain. Blinding geometry unfolds from inside it. The Silence begins.

description: Shatter the Ascended: Silence.

actions.message: The god-form collapses, but the universe keeps breaking. Vale's code still blocks the signal, and the Key will only answer a circuit, not a ruler.

description: Link the crew through the Key and tune the world as separate voices.
Find the Aethel Grave.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Aethel Grave

Orion hears the first Architects resting inside the current, not erased but carried. Ancestral Grace unlocked.

description: Recover the future Astra hull.

actions.message:
Quest Complete: The Final Scavenge

Zeke extracts the future Astra fragment and refuses the timeline it came from. The Starborn mod is not proof the ship dies; it is proof the crew can still change the ending.

description: Replay the epilogue.

actions.message: You gather charred starleaf for trail cooking.
You harvest luminous mushroom caps.
You gather resonant reed bulbs.
You gather bitter blue fronds.
You dress the tidepool crawler for camp.
You salvage clean meat from the echo nest.
You preserve grazer meat for a trail meal.
You recover a clean cut from the shard-hound nest.
The falls mask the campfire. Food, quiet, and a Neural Stabilizer ease the pressure of Erosion.
The heat ring holds steady while your thoughts settle back into one rhythm.
The party rests, cooks, and listens until the Ocean feels distant enough to bear.
The sterile heat plate becomes a campsite; the Neural Stabilizer anchors your mind while you rest.
Lost Patrol started: the first signal points deeper into the Wilds.
First distress signature triangulated.
Second distress signature decoded.
Third signature confirms the patrol cache location.
Tideglass reflections corroborate the patrol vector toward the ridge.
The patrol cache holds a cracked Thermal Cutter, a repair schematic, and enough salvage to rebuild the handle. Nova will have to make it usable before it can cut razor-vines.

Quest Complete: Lost Patrol

Nova rebuilds the Thermal Cutter from Dominion salvage. The heated edge can cauterize Sector 9 razor-vines before they burst.
The west resonance crystal settles into a low supporting voice. The mural will need the answering east voice and the bright north voice before it can sing safely.
The east resonance crystal answers the west voice with a cleaner harmonic. The mural sockets brighten without flattening into one note.
The north resonance crystal adds a high stabilizing voice. Three tones now hold apart like Ice in dark water.

Quest Complete: Ancient Echoes

Three crystal voices align the stasis mural. The song remembers humans as Ice held safely within the Ocean.
Gh0st folds your signal into the grid noise. Security is blind for ninety seconds.
You pass between the guard-system sweeps without raising an alert.
Beyond the disabled grid, you recover the transmitter core. Gh0st strips the timing crystal and sends you a capacitor schematic terse enough to count as a threat.

Quest Complete: Stolen Tech

Nova assembles the Rapid Capacitor from stolen Dominion timing hardware. Gh0st approves by not correcting anything.
The grotto crystals are inert. Orion's Source Resin comes from living tideglass materials, not a finished relic waiting on a pedestal.
The reliquary pedestal is keyed to the facility chorus. It will not release anything until the mural chord is stable.
The pod is not a prison. It is a pressure shell, keeping Orion's memories distinct while the Source pushes at them from every side.
The overview shows three sockets around the chamber mural. Each socket corresponds to a ring: memory, pressure, and return.
Nova opens the coolant garden by a fraction. The pipes stop shuddering, and the rings lose their fever-bright edge.
The ring console refuses blind input. The chamber needs a read pattern, pod pressure map, overview socket map, and stable coolant flow before the rings can move safely.
Orion braces the harmonic horn while Nova loosens the copper stays. The left band stops screaming and begins to breathe.
The receiver cup cools as Gh0st grounds the casing. Its white-hot glow drops into a steady blue pulse.
The root pressure gauge reveals the trick: the barrier is not powered by the roots, it is choking them. The pressure needs a bleed, not a cut.
Nova throws the generator breakers in a staggered pattern. The gate keeps glowing, but the last band loses the power to bite.
The acoustic lock howls in three clashing bands. Orion can hear the resolving chord better than the console can display it.

description: Restores Nova at her bunk.

actions.message: Nova rests in the narrow bunk until the colony hum settles into something almost gentle.

description: Restores the party at the Homestead mess hall.

actions.message: A hot ration and the mess hall's ugly old rhythm steady the body for another push.

description: Restores the party at the Source memory oasis.

actions.message: The crew eats around the blue fire while names, jokes, and old fears circle close enough to hold for one more breath.

description: Restores the party before the final Source ascent.

actions.message: The last meal is simple, warm, and already fraying at the edges of Nova's memory.

description: Restores the active party aboard the Astra.

actions.message: The crew rests aboard the Astra. Wounds close, nerves settle, and the ship feels like home for a while.
